what_it_does: Defines a Workshop UX polish layer with a keyboard shortcuts modal, a first-run Vi onboarding tour stored in localStorage, and a React error boundary recovery panel, then exposes the three UI units on window.
observations: Uses React state/effects via aliased globals useState_p and useEffect_p.
The shortcuts overlay opens from ? or Shift+/ and closes from Escape, backdrop click, or the close button.
Shortcut content is static data grouped into Navigate, Edit, Vi gallery, and Vi everywhere sections.
The onboarding tour appears once unless cvstudio:onboarded:engine-v2 exists in localStorage.
Onboarding steps explain field alternatives, block proposals, document transforms, chat-driven edits, and reusable templates.
The error boundary stores err/info, logs caught errors, and renders reload and retry controls.
Exports are assigned to window rather than module exports.
standouts: Combines keyboard help, first-run education, and render-error recovery in one file-local polish layer.
Uses static content arrays as the data source for both shortcut and onboarding UI.
Frames Vi capabilities as embedded controls across fields, dividers, transforms, chat, and templates.
conventions: Functional React components are used for modal-style UI.
Class-based React error boundary is used because render error boundaries require class lifecycle APIs in this style.
Backdrop click closes the shortcuts overlay while panel click stops propagation.
localStorage access is wrapped in try/catch to tolerate unavailable storage.
Global window assignment is used as the exposure pattern.
concerns: The file depends on ambient globals such as React, ViShape, localStorage, location, and window.
The keydown listener is recreated when open changes because open is in the effect dependency list.
The shortcuts guard ignores input, textarea, and contentEditable targets, but not other editable controls such as select.
Retrying the error boundary only clears local boundary state, so a still-throwing child will immediately trigger the fallback again.
notes: This file is the Workshop polish surface for discoverability, onboarding, and graceful render-error recovery.
questions: Where are the window-exposed components mounted?
Is the onboarding key versioned intentionally as engine-v2 for resetting prior onboarding state?
Should the shortcuts overlay be available before or after onboarding appears?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it provides a keyboard shortcuts overlay, first-run onboarding, and a render error boundary.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make Workshop’s Vi-assisted editing surfaces discoverable while preserving continuity if a subtree crashes.
novelty: The onboarding and shortcuts copy treat Vi actions as available across multiple substrate types: fields, dividers, whole documents, chat diffs, and templates.
contribution: Contributes a file-local interaction layer for shortcut discovery, first-run Vi orientation, and recoverable rendering failures that a fused system would keep as UX scaffolding around editing canvases.
summary_for_embedding: This is a React polish file for Workshop that defines a shortcuts overlay, a one-time Vi onboarding tour, and an error boundary recovery panel exposed on window.
intention_for_embedding: It was for helping users discover Vi-assisted Workshop controls and keeping the interface usable after render errors.

symbols.does: Renders a keyboard shortcuts modal when toggled by the ? shortcut.
symbols.description: This component owns the shortcuts overlay open state and installs a window keydown listener. It ignores text-editing targets, toggles visibility from ? or Shift+/, and renders grouped shortcut rows from the SHORTCUTS array with backdrop and close-button dismissal.

symbols.does: Handles global keyboard input for opening and closing the shortcuts overlay.
symbols.description: The handler exits early for input, textarea, and contentEditable targets. It prevents default behavior when toggling the overlay from ? or Shift+/ and closes the overlay on Escape when it is open.

symbols.does: Renders a one-time multi-step onboarding panel for Vi Workshop capabilities.
symbols.description: This component checks localStorage for the onboarding completion key and starts at the first step when no completion value is present. It renders the current ONBOARD_STEPS entry, progress dots with click and keyboard activation, a skip action, and a CTA that advances through the sequence.

symbols.does: Marks onboarding complete and hides the onboarding panel.
symbols.description: This function writes the ONBOARD_KEY value to localStorage inside a try/catch, then sets the step state to -1. It is used by the Skip button and by the final onboarding step.

symbols.does: Advances onboarding or dismisses it at the final step.
symbols.description: This function compares the current step against the last ONBOARD_STEPS index. It either increments the step state or calls dismiss when the tour has reached its final entry.

symbols.does: Catches React render errors in its child subtree and displays a recovery fallback.
symbols.description: This class component stores caught error state through getDerivedStateFromError and componentDidCatch, logs error details, and renders children while no error exists. When an error is present, it shows a Workshop recovery panel with the message, a reload action, and a retry action that clears the boundary state.
